teamObjective.explanation: I am in an endgame race with only 2 cards left, the fewest at the table. Leading the lower 2 of spades keeps my Ace as a reserve to contest a later trick and advances my plan to empty my hand.

partnerBelief.summary: No public information about partner's cards is available at the start of the game.

opponentBelief.summary: No public information about opponents' cards is available at the start of the game.

actionRationale.primaryReason: I selected a legal play of the single 2 of spades. In an endgame lead, playing the lower card first preserves the Ace as a higher reserve card that can beat a later single-card lead and keeps me in the race to finish.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Playing the Ace first would spend my only high card; if an opponent takes control, I would only have the weak 2 left and might lose the race.

risks: An opponent with a higher non-trump single or any trump may beat the 2 of spades and take control.
I may not get another lead if the opponent plays a combination I cannot match with my remaining Ace.
riskAssessment.mitigation: I keep the Ace of spades as a reserve to regain control if the next trick is a single card within its rank range.
